entries.definition: A sudden brief burst of bright light or flame; a device producing a bright light as a signal or for illumination.
entries.exampleSentence: They fired a distress flare to alert the rescue team.

entries.definition: To blaze or burn with a sudden intensity; to burst out or spread suddenly; to become wider or more open (of clothing).
entries.exampleSentence: Tempers flared during the heated discussion.

entries.definition: A sudden eruption of energy on the surface of the sun (solar flare).
entries.exampleSentence: A powerful solar flare disrupted radio communications.
